qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: OAuth open redirect via unrestricted default redirect URI policy in OAuthProxy
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding concerns open redirect via unrestricted OAuth redirect URI validation in redirect_validation.py and proxy.py. The advisory describes SSRF and path traversal via unencoded path parameter substitution in the _build_url() method of fastmcp/utilities/openapi/director.py. Different vulnerable code, different root cause, different vulnerability class.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: ReDoS via user-controlled regex pattern in RegexSearchTransform search_tools
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding concerns ReDoS via user-controlled regex in search_tools (regex.py). The advisory describes SSRF and path traversal via _build_url() in the OpenAPI Provider's RequestDirector (director.py). Different vulnerable code, different root cause, different vulnerability class.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The advisory describes SSRF and path traversal in the _build_url() method of fastmcp/utilities/openapi/director.py, where path parameters are substituted without URL encoding and urljoin() resolves ../ sequences. Neither qualifying finding identifies this vulnerable code path. The oauth-open-redirect-default finding addresses unrestricted OAuth redirect URI validation (different files, different mechanism). The redos-via-regex-search finding addresses catastrophic regex backtracking in search_tools (different file, different mechanism).
evidence: Advisory root cause: direct path parameter substitution without URL encoding + urljoin() ../ traversal
Finding oauth-open-redirect-default: redirect_validation.py and proxy.py - OAuth redirect URI validation
Finding redos-via-regex-search: regex.py - re.compile() with user-controlled pattern
